candidate: SNAP-ATTN: phase-coherent attention burst via multi-layer latch-accumulator + threshold-triggered sharp-τ release (pistol shrimp cavitation latch-and-snap).

per_result_judgments.reason: Source-domain shrimp biology page; no LLM-side overlap on any axis.
Optimal attention temperature for ICL: touches sharp-τ axis (1 kw) but lacks accumulator/burst-layer mechanism; all three axes below threshold.
Spiking transformer with spatial-temporal accumulator: sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7. Same functional family of accumulator+spike attention.
Loss-spike management (Spike No More): about training-loss spikes, not controlled attention burst; all axes below threshold.
SpikeLLM saliency-based spiking: func>=0.7 (saliency-spike accumulator + release in same family).
Accumulative-context fine-tuning for temporal forecasting; accumulator concept but no impulsive sharp-τ burst layer; all axes below threshold.
Direct twin: 'Multi-Layer Latch Accumulator with Impulsive Sharp-τ Attention Burst' matches all four LLM-side content words; all three axes far above threshold.
Adaptive temperature burst layers selected via accumulator: matches sharp-temperature burst + occasional impulsive burst layer + inter-layer accumulator; all three axes above threshold.

summary: Four of eight retrieved results clear the hit-rule, including two near-direct twins (ranks 7 and 8) that explicitly describe a multi-layer accumulator triggering a sharp-temperature attention burst layer. Ranks 3 and 5 establish the broader spiking/saliency-accumulator family that SNAP-ATTN sits within. The distinctive multi-layer-latch + impulsive-sharp-τ release combination is already covered in the prior literature, so the candidate is high-overlap and not novel.
